rationale: Counterexample (if any) to Erdős-Gyárfás 1995 would disprove a 31-year-old conjecture (CS=disproof_closure). Mechanism is exhibit-and-decide: provide explicit Fin n SimpleGraph adjacency, decide G.minDegree >= 3, decide non-existence of 4-cycle, 8-cycle, 16-cycle via Mathlib SimpleGraph.Walk.IsCycle decidability (CM=disproof_construction). Aristotle's S6 graph-counterexample DNA (slots 114/131/250/267 Tuza ν=4 arc) is a structural match. CR primary=clean_decidable: G.minDegree and cycle length are computable for Fin n graphs with DecidableRel. CR secondary=witness_search_explosion: enumerating all SimpleGraph (Fin 25) is 2^300 — Aristotle cannot search; we (external SAT) provide the witness, Aristotle verifies. HM=journal_clean: a counterexample to Erdős-Gyárfás is a journal-headline result.

phases: PHASE 1: random 3-regular graphs n in {18..25}, ~16k trials
PHASE 2: random min-deg-3 graphs m in [3n/2, 2n], ~48k trials
PHASE 3: random min-deg-3 graphs m in [2n, 2.5n], ~32k trials
PHASE 4: hill-climbing 4-cycle removal on cubic graphs n in {18..32}, ~1.6k trials with up to 100 swaps each
external_search.fragment_observation: Of ~120 random cubic graphs that hill-climbing brought to 4-cycle-free form across n ∈ {18,20,22,24,26,28,30,32}, ALL contained an 8-cycle. Consistent with Hu-Shen 2024 / Hegde et al Feb 2025 lit bounds.
external_search.honest_assessment: Random sampling at n ≤ 32 is incomplete; a real CNF SAT run on cadical/kissat could push to n ≈ 40 where a counterexample, if any, is most likely to live. The negative search result HOLD-signals the literature but does not rigorously prove non-existence at n ≤ 25 — that requires either (a) Aristotle's decide closing the impossibility direction, or (b) an exhaustive SAT search with proof certificate. The submitted sketch is the existence direction; if Aristotle returns sorry / no witness, this is a meaningful HOLD signal but not a closure of the impossibility direction.

advance_signature_match: S6 (explicit small graph counterexample — Tuza ν=4 arc slots 114/131/250/267)
aristotle_prior_history: No prior Erdős 64 submission. Tuza arc (slots 114, 131, 250, 267) demonstrates Aristotle CAN verify supplied small-graph counterexamples via decide on adjacency + Finset enumeration. The gap here is: can Aristotle synthesize the counterexample without one being supplied? S6 DNA suggests YES for n ≤ 13; n ∈ {18..25} is at the outer edge of the demonstrated scale.
feasibility_notes: C7 mechanism analyst ranked feasibility 7/10 (Aristotle CAN verify). C10 ranked closure probability 3/10. Closure probability 3 here because: external search found nothing (8-cycle constraint is binding at all sampled n); without a supplied witness, Aristotle's S6 DNA does not extend cleanly to discovery in this regime. If Aristotle's compute is allocated to a cycle-length-DAG enumeration this could succeed; if it tries random adjacency synthesis it will likely fail.
expected_outcome: compiled_partial OR compiled_no_advance with sorry on the existence claim; small chance of compiled_advance if Aristotle's enumeration synthesizes a witness; vanishingly small chance of a real disproof of Erdős-Gyárfás 1995.
submission_action: DRAFT ONLY — not yet submitted. Submit only after agent #5 of 5 review.
fallback_rationale: External search found no counterexample. Original plan (embed witness graph as def witness : SimpleGraph (Fin n)) cannot be executed without a witness. Fallback: state the existence question as a bare gap at the bounded scope n ≤ 25, let Aristotle attempt synthesis.
